title: Boat Rental in Walvis Bay: Costs and Options for 2026
html:
Walvis Bay, on Namibia’s Skeleton Coast, offers sheltered lagoon boating with regular sightings of flamingos and dolphins. Boat rentals from the Waterfront Jetty typically cost between 90 and 550 EUR per half-day, and BoatHire24 lists verified options for every budget.

How much does it cost to rent a boat in Walvis Bay?

Rental prices range from 20 EUR for a shared lagoon tour to 900 EUR for a crewed luxury yacht. Costs vary by boat size, duration, and whether you skipper yourself or hire a crew. For the best value, consider a small motorboat half-day from 90 EUR or a catamaran full-day from 350 EUR.

  • Shared lagoon tour: 20, 40 EUR per person (2, 3 hours)
  • Small motorboat half-day: 90, 150 EUR (4, 6 guests)
  • Catamaran full-day: 350, 550 EUR (8, 12 guests)
  • Crewed yacht full-day: 600, 900 EUR (up to 10 guests)

You can hire a boat directly through BoatHire24. Prices include safety equipment and basic briefing.

Which boat to rent in Walvis Bay?

Four main boat types suit different activities: sightseeing, fishing, or luxury cruising. Each has a recommended capacity and indicative daily rate.

  1. Rib or speedboat, ideal for dolphin watching and Pelican Point trips (4, 6 guests, from 120 EUR half-day)
  2. Fishing boat, equipped for deep-sea angling off Sandwich Harbour (6, 8 guests, from 200 EUR half-day)
  3. Catamaran, stable platform for lagoon cruises and sundowner trips (8, 12 guests, from 350 EUR full-day)
  4. Motor yacht, for example the Princess V58 for hire, luxury with cabin, suitable for overnights (6, 8 guests, from 500 EUR half-day)
  5. Sailing yacht, silent cruising on the lagoon, ideal for birdwatching (4, 6 guests, from 250 EUR full-day)

Where to set off in Walvis Bay?

Four main departure points connect you to the best cruising grounds: the Waterfront, the Lagoon, Pelican Point, and Sandwich Harbour. Walvis Bay Waterfront has a marina with fuel and provisioning. The Lagoon, a Ramsar wetland, is a no-wake zone where you can drift past flamingos. Pelican Point, 15 nautical miles south, is a seal colony and surf break. Sandwich Harbour, 30 nautical miles south, offers lagoons and dunes.

BoatHire24 listings include pickup points at Walvis Bay Waterfront and Swakopmund jetty, the nearest town 30 km north.

Do you need a boat licence in Walvis Bay?

For powerboats over 15 hp you need a valid boating licence, but many charters provide a skipper. Rental operators confirm exact requirements per boat. A local day skipper costs around 100 EUR extra.

Book a catamaran at sunrise: the lagoon is glassy calm, dolphins often feed right off Pelican Point, and you avoid the afternoon south-westerly wind that picks up after 14:00.

At a glance

  • Price range: 20, 900 EUR
  • Peak months: November to March (Namibian summer)
  • Trip length: 2 hours to full day
  • Capacity: 4, 12 guests on most boats
  • Currency: EUR (quoted in this article)

Questions about boat rental in Walvis Bay

Can I rent a boat without a licence in Walvis Bay?

Yes, many operators include a skipper, or you can hire one separately so you do not need your own licence.

What month has the best weather for boating in Walvis Bay?

November through March offers the calmest seas, with average temperatures of 20, 25°C and light south-westerly winds under 15 knots.

Is Walvis Bay safe for sailing?

The lagoon is sheltered and well-charted, but always check local conditions with your operator before heading beyond the breakwater.

How to book your Walvis Bay boat rental

BoatHire24 lets you compare listings, read verified reviews, and book securely. Use the boat search to filter by date, guest count, and boat type. Instant booking options are available on many boats.

Walvis Bay offers some of Namibia’s most accessible boating, with a mix of wildlife, dunes, and ocean. Whether you prefer a fast rib or a luxury motor yacht, BoatHire24 is a worldwide marketplace where you can rent a boat in Walvis Bay or rent out your boat to other travellers.

Written by the BoatHire24 team, who book and list verified boats in Walvis Bay and hundreds of destinations worldwide.
